hook.text: Spotify doesn't organize music by genre alone. It uses moods, activities, decades, and personal taste profiles.

buildup.text: Traditional music stores used genre hierarchies: Rock > Classic Rock > 1970s. Spotify broke that model entirely.

discovery.text: Playlists like 'Chill Hits,' 'Workout Beats,' and 'Dinner Jazz' organize music by context of use, not by musicology.

twist.text: User-created playlists became the primary IA. Millions of users organized Spotify's catalog better than editors could.

climax.text: Activity-based architecture works when the same item fits many contexts. A song is for working out AND for driving.

punchline.text: Organize by use, not type. Context beats category for experiential content.

quiz.question: How does Spotify's information architecture differ from traditional music stores?
options: It organizes by mood, activity, and context instead of strict genre hierarchies
Spotify removed all categories and relies solely on search
